title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home with our easy step-by-step recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful Indian classic!
longTailKeywords: butter chicken recipe step by step
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani
Variations and Substitutions for Murgh Makhani
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy, and authentic flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian curry featuring tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Its mild spice profile and luscious texture make it a beloved dish for all palates, perfect for a comforting meal. This classic recipe promises an authentic taste of India right in your kitchen.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 mins

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, 1 tsp garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or kadai over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Add canned crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, cumin powder, coriander powder, 1 tsp garam masala, and salt to the pan. Mix well and bring to a simmer. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the pan from heat. Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of water. For an extra smooth sauce, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces and stir to combine. Add sugar, kasuri methi (crushed between your palms), and the remaining 2 tbsp butter. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Add Cream and Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and heat through gently for 1-2 minutes, do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasonings if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate overnight. Grilling or baking the chicken before adding to the sauce adds a smoky flavor.

Adjust spice levels by increasing or decreasing red chili powder. For a richer sauce, use full-fat cream and a generous amount of butter. Soaking cashews is crucial for a smooth, creamy sauce without adding too much fat from cream.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as flavors deepen. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
article:
Butter Chicken, known traditionally as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global culinary phenomenon. This iconic Indian chicken recipe has captured hearts and palates worldwide with its irresistibly rich, creamy, and subtly sweet tomato-based gravy. Originating from the bustling streets of Delhi, it has become a staple in Indian restaurants and homes, celebrated for its tender pieces of chicken swimming in a luxurious, aromatic sauce. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality Butter Chicken experience in your own kitchen, you're in the right place. Get ready to embark on a flavorful journey to master this classic, transforming simple ingredients into an extraordinary feast.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, translates directly to "chicken with butter," a name that perfectly encapsulates its defining characteristics. Its history dates back to the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, where Kundan Lal Gujral innovated this dish. Legend has it that he created the gravy to rehydrate leftover tandoori chicken, resulting in a revolutionary new flavor profile.

At its core, Murgh Makhani features succulent pieces of chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices, then grilled or pan-fried until tender. The real magic, however, lies in its signature gravy. This velvety sauce is a harmonious blend of pureed tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a medley of aromatic Indian spices like garam masala, cumin, and coriander. What truly sets it apart is the generous amount of butter and fresh cream incorporated into the gravy, lending it an unparalleled richness and a smooth, luxurious texture. A touch of kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) adds a subtle, earthy bitterness that balances the sweetness and acidity, making Butter Chicken an exquisite symphony of flavors. It’s a dish that embodies comfort, elegance, and the vibrant spirit of Indian cuisine.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken
Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients for both the flavorful chicken marinade and the luscious gravy.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   2 cups canned crushed tomatoes (or 4-5 fresh ripe tomatoes, pureed)
*   1/2 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon sugar or honey (adjust to taste)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   1 tablespoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Instructions
Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken that will impress even the most discerning palates.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor absorption.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until the chicken is lightly charred and cooked through. Remove from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Build the Gravy Flavor:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews (if using), turmeric powder, red chili powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and garam masala. Mix well. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the oil starts to separate.
5.  **Blend the Gravy:** Carefully transfer the cooked gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid. Discard any remaining solids.
6.  **Finish the Murgh Makhani:** Return the smooth gravy to the pan over low-medium heat. Stir in the sugar or honey, kasuri methi (crushed between your palms), and salt to taste. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy.
7.  **Add Cream and Simmer:** Pour in the heavy cream and gently stir until combined. Let the Butter Chicken simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich sauce. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream.
8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and an optional swirl of extra cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Murgh Makhani
Achieving restaurant-quality Murgh Makhani at home is within reach with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination yields the best results.
*   **Char the Chicken:** A slight char on the chicken pieces before adding them to the gravy adds depth and a smoky flavor that is characteristic of authentic Butter Chicken, mimicking the tandoor oven effect.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy base thoroughly and then straining it through a fine-mesh sieve is crucial for that luxurious, lump-free texture. Don't skip this step if you desire a truly silky sauce.
*   **Use Quality Ingredients:** Fresh, ripe tomatoes (or good quality canned crushed tomatoes), full-fat cream, and real butter make a significant difference in the final taste and richness.
*   **Kasuri Methi:** Always crush the dried fenugreek leaves between your palms before adding them to the gravy. This releases their aromatic oils, intensifying their unique flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Butter Chicken
Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, turning a simple meal into a lavish spread.

*   **Indian Breads:** The most classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Roti, paratha, or even laccha paratha are also excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed Basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast to the creamy sauce and helps absorb the flavors.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad, cooling raita (yogurt dip), or some sliced red onion and lemon wedges can offer a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Murgh Makhani.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish with a sprinkle of fresh chopped coriander and a swirl of heavy cream for visual appeal and an extra touch of decadence.

## Variations and Substitutions for Murgh Makhani
While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is beloved, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary preferences or explore new flavor profiles.

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** For a delicious vegetarian option, substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) to make Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu or chickpeas.
*   **Vegan Version:** To make this dish vegan, replace butter with plant-based butter or oil, and use full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of dairy cream. Ensure the yogurt in the marinade is plant-based, or omit it and marinate in lemon juice and spices.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your preference. For a milder dish, reduce it; for more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Nut Alternatives:** If you have a cashew allergy, you can use blanched almonds for creaminess, or simply omit the nuts and rely more on cream for richness.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic smoky taste without a tandoor, you can use the dhungar method: place a small bowl with a burning charcoal piece in the gravy, drizzle ghee over it, and cover for a few minutes.

Butter Chicken is a culinary masterpiece that promises a delightful experience with every bite. By following these detailed steps and tips, you can confidently recreate this iconic Indian dish at home, bringing the warmth and richness of Murgh Makhani to your own dining table. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and relish the incredible flavors of this truly classic Indian chicken recipe.
